strengths: Built production ELT pipelines with Airflow and dbt
Quantified impact on efficiency and data accuracy
weaknesses: No explicit LLM/agentic AI tooling experience
AWS/Azure and broader distributed systems detail lacking
No mention of machine-readable schemas or API design practices
Security tooling and guardrail automation not shown
improvement_bullets: Integrated LLM-based automation for data pipeline monitoring, utilizing agentic workflows to proactively flag anomalies and reduce data quality validation time by 30%.
Designed and documented machine-readable schemas and clean REST APIs for internal data services, enabling seamless integration for cross-functional teams and non-technical users.
Engineered CI/CD guardrails and security scanners within GitHub Actions to automate code quality checks, ensuring maintainability across production-grade ETL pipelines.
Collaborated in a 0-to-1 environment at Haven Technologies, bridging the gap between clinical-style business logic and technical implementation to accelerate feature delivery by 25%.
Developed robust data infrastructure on AWS and GCP, focusing on distributed systems optimization to support high-scale drug development data modeling and retrieval-augmented pipelines.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Software Engineer (Open Level) opportunity with Formation Bio. I am interested in this opportunity because of Formation Bio’s unique mission to break the drug development bottleneck by merging advanced technology with clinical expertise. What interests me about working for Formation Bio is your vision for AI-native pharma, specifically the integration of agentic coding systems to accelerate clinical research, which inspires me because it directly applies my passion for data engineering to the pursuit of life-saving medicines. I believe my expertise in production-grade ELT pipelines and my proficiency in building collaborative, user-centric tools would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on the intersection of data quality and operational efficiency. In fact, while building the FRED Economic Batch Pipeline, I implemented a full ELT stack using Python, Airflow, and dbt to ingest complex financial data; I utilized Great Expectations to build automated guardrails, which reduced analyst query time and ensured high-integrity datasets. Ultimately, I know that the Software Engineer (Open Level) role at Formation Bio would allow me to bring my experience in building scalable infrastructure to your mission of accelerating drug discovery. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my cross-functional collaboration skills. I worked directly with product managers and domain experts to translate ambiguous requirements into production code, successfully driving a 15% increase in feature adoption while improving data accuracy by 60%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python, SQL, and cloud infrastructure to work on building the next generation of drug development tools at Formation Bio. I know that my technical rigor and ability to thrive in ambiguous, fast-moving environments align with what it takes to succeed at the Software Engineer (Open Level) role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
